item.title: Mauritius weather bulletin – 24 March 2026
item.description: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 16h30 on Tuesday 24 March 2026.General situation:1. Few clouds coming from the Eastern sector will influence the local weather as from tonight.2. A light and warm airstream is flowing over the region.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Partly cloudy in general tonight and tomorrow morning becoming cloudy at times to the East and over the highgrounds with showers.The minimum temperature will be between 21 and 23 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 25 and 27 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Cloud developments are expected tomorrow afternoon over the Western part of the island and over the Central Plateau with localised showers. These showers may be temporarily moderate.Partly cloudy elsewhere.The maximum temperature will be slightly above the mean and will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 34 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.Wind will blow from the Eastern sector at about 15 km/h.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with Southwesterly swells of the order of 2 metres.High tides :17h47 and tomorrow 04h04.Low tides :23h20 and tomorrow 12h28.Sunset :18h18.Sunrise (tomorrow) :06h15.Atmospheric pressure at 16h00:1013 hectopascals.
item.titles: Mauritius weather bulletin – 24 March 2026

item.title: In US, thousands of Cuban migrants who arrived legally fear deportation
item.description: Cubans, who have historically received preferential treatment from the US, have not been spared by President Donald Trump's war on immigration. The US has ended family reunification and legal immigration from Cuba by adding the island to its travel ban. It has also suspended temporary protection programmes, exposing thousands of people who arrived legally to deportation to a country suffering from severe US sanctions and crippling electricity blackouts.
item.titles: In US, thousands of Cuban migrants who arrived legally fear deportation

item.title: March shoulder season in Europe and Asia: Fewer crowds – more opportunities
item.description: Experienced travelers know that planning a truly memorable vacation and choosing the right timing is not easy. While some tourists organize trips during the peak season and complain about crowded streets, others take full advantage of the March shoulder season. During this period, flights and hotels are usually more affordable, queues are almost nonexistent, and cities live in their usual rhythm without the overwhelming tourist crowds.
item.titles: March shoulder season in Europe and Asia: Fewer crowds – more opportunities

item.title: Afghanistan's Taliban government releases detained US national Dennis Coyle
item.description: Afghanistan’s Taliban government said on Tuesday it had released American academic Dennis Coyle after more than a year in detention. Coyle was arrested in January 2025 on allegations he violated laws, although Afghan authorities never publicly stated what laws he was accused of violating.
item.titles: Afghanistan's Taliban government releases detained US national Dennis Coyle

The growing popularity of the annual spring celebrations organised around the blooms is proving to be a boon for local farmers and the tourism industry.

item.title: 'Paris started with an openness to queer people': Author Rasheed Newson
item.description: A leading author is speaking in Paris about the French capital's ability to draw gay and bisexual people to it. Rasheed Newson's talk is entitled "From Baldwin to Today: Queer Black Expats in Paris". He says that many queer African Americans seeking freedom and creative possibility have come to Paris due to its openness to queer people. Newson is the author of the bestseller "My Government Means to Kill Me", and has a new book is coming out shortly, "There’s Only One Sin in Hollywood: A Novel".
item.titles: 'Paris started with an openness to queer people': Author Rasheed Newson

item.title: Ivory Coast heritage sites under threat despite significant discoveries
item.description: In the Ivory Coast, archaeologists are sounding the alarm as coastal heritage sites are threatened by urbanisation and soil exploitation. These areas have no special protection despite significant artefacts being uncovered during earlier excavations - later halted due to a lack of funding. FRANCE 24's team reports from the ground
item.titles: Ivory Coast heritage sites under threat despite significant discoveries

item.title: Nigerian refinery begins fuel exports to Africa
item.description:
[France 24] In tonight's edition: Nigeria's Dangote Refinery says it's stepped up exports across Africa as the war in Iran squeezes traditional fuel supply routes and disrupts energy flows. Also, 76 percent of births in sub-Saharan Africa occur in healthcare facilities without basic water, sanitation and hygiene, according to a new report from the NGO WaterAid. Plus archeologists in the Ivory Coast raise concerns about coastal heritage sites that they say are under threat due to soil exploitation and urbani
[France 24] Nigeria's Dangote Petroleum Refinery has stepped up gasoline exports across Africa as disruptions to energy flows due to the Iran conflict squeeze traditional ​fuel ‌supply routes, curbing the cheap imports that long dominated West African ⁠markets. As the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, the refinery could also see a surge in European demand, especially for jet fuel. FRANCE 24's Clemence Waller has more.
[Africanews] Nigeria's Dangote mega-refinery says it has begun exporting fuel to other African countries, after global oil prices soared due to the Middle East war.
item.titles: Nigerian refinery begins fuel exports to Africa Nigeria's Dangote refinery increases exports as Middle East war shakes global markets Dangote refinery says begun fuel exports in Africa

This shows 'Hezbollah and Iran are still able to coordinate strikes', says FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky from Jerusalem.

item.title: Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty
item.description:
[BBC News] Australia and the EU sign sweeping trade and security deals after years of negotiations.
[France 24] The European Union and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on March 24, while also agreeing to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. It's the latest Brussels has agreed to, to diversify trade. But the deal was slammed by EU farmers, who have called 'unacceptable'. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating has more from Brussels.
[France 24] The EU and Australia have signed a sweeping free trade agreement that will eliminate tariffs on nearly all trade between them as global economies seek to hedge against chaotic US tariff policies. Also in this edition: crude oil prices rise again as traders remain sceptical of signals that Washington is trying to de-escalate its war with Iran.
item.titles: Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty EU signs trade deal with Australia as it looks for new partners amid tension with US EU, Australia seal free trade deal eight years in the making

item.title: Search for four missing soldiers after Colombia plane crash kills 66
item.description:
[BBC News] The Hercules military transport plane crashed minutes after take-off near Colombia's southern border with Peru.
[France 24] A military transport plane crashed in Colombia on March 24, killing at least 66 passengers. The Lockheed Martin Hercules C-130 plane that reportedly suffered an impact near the runway as it took off from a a city near the Peruvian border, fell from the sky in a remote area, impeding rescue efforts. The event prompted calls from Colombian President Gustavo Petro to modernise the country's ageing military--a process he says has been hamstrung by bureaucratic obstacles.
[France 24] A military transport plane with 125 people on board, mostly soldiers, crashed shortly after taking off Monday in Puerto Leguizamo, Colombia, killing at least 66 people and leaving dozens injured, the head of Colombia’s armed forces said.
item.titles: Search for four missing soldiers after Colombia plane crash kills 66 Colombian military plane crashes, killing at least 66 At least 66 killed in military plane crash in Colombia

item.title: WMO warns 'ocean warming at record levels & will continue for hundreds, if not thousands, of years'
item.description: Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Claire Ransom, coordinator of the WMO's State of the Global Climate 2025 report. Ransom warns that the planet is not only warming, but entering a state of energy imbalance. Driven by record concentrations of greenhouse gases, climate change is intensifying across interconnected systems: oceans, cryosphere, and atmosphere. At the heart of this shift is the ocean, now absorbing the vast majority of excess heat. This process is not only accelerating sea-level rise
item.titles: WMO warns 'ocean warming at record levels & will continue for hundreds, if not thousands, of years'

item.title: Unclear if Trump is telling truth about talks with Iran or if it's 'bluff'
item.description:
[France 24] US President Donald Trump has said that the US and Iran are having 'very good' talks regarding the war. But they were denied by Tehran, although they did say they had received a request from the White House. There is also the question of where talks would be held - with Pakistan coming out as the most likely location. But the major question is: 'is Trump telling the truth about points of agreement, or is it bluff?' asks FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani.
[Al Jazeera] Iranian officials have dismissed statements by Trump as a ‘bluff’, denying any direct or indirect talks with the US.
[Africanews] President Donald Trump said Monday the U.S. was talking with a “top" and "respected” Iranian leader and claimed the Islamic Republic was eager for a deal to end the war.
item.titles: Unclear if Trump is telling truth about talks with Iran or if it's 'bluff' Iran says no talks with US, calls Trump statements a ‘bluff’ Trump claims ‘very strong’ Iran talks as Tehran denies contact

item.description: Deux courses, deux victoires. Voilà le bilan de Hanson Matombé en ce début de saison 2026. En effet, le coureur du Faucon Flacq SC-KFC se retrouve en échappée avec un représentant du Vélo Club des Jeunes Cyclistes de Curepipe-Palco et s’impose au sprint final. item.description: Le lundi 23 mars 2026, la *State and Other Employees Federation (SEF)* a tenu une conférence de presse à l’Université de Maurice afin de dénoncer les dysfonctionnements persistants dans la fonction publique, les retards de paiement dans le secteur de la santé ainsi que la mauvaise gestion des fonds publics. item.title: En Italie : Cyclisme : Week-end compliqué pour Kimberley Le Court-Pienaar
item.description: Week-end compliqué en Italie pour Kimberley Le Court-Pienaar. Victime d’une chute alors qu’elle figurait parmi les prétendantes sur Milan-Sanremo, samedi la Mauricienne a vu ses ambitions brutalement stoppées. Le lendemain, au Giro dell'Appennino Donne, elle a tenté de rebondir en se montrant active dans le final. La leader de AG Insurance-Soudal a démontré sa combativité mais n’a pu jouer la gagne mais elle en sort avec de riches enseignements à n’en pas douter. Parmi les fav ...

item.title: Bébé Ezekiel : Sous assistance respiratoire après sept heures d’intervention au bloc
item.description: Il fallu quasiment sept heures d’intervention chirurgicale pour le petit Ezekiel Aaron Cleo. Bonne nouvelle : l’opération qu’il a subie en Inde, hier, s’est bien déroulée. Selon les informations communiquées par le Dr Naveen Chaudhri de l’équipe OMCA India, l’opération a été réalisée à l’hôpital Max à Saket, New Delhi, par le Dr Kulbhushan S. item.description: Le championnat de division 2A est des plus palpitants. En effet, l’on a droit à des matches disputés et à rebondissements à chaque journée. Aujourd’hui, se disputent les derniers matches comptant pour la phase préliminaire et les protagonistes veulent la conclure de la meilleure manière qui soit. item.description: Avec sa nouvelle rubrique «Parlement citoyen – Readers Question Time», l’express renforce son engagement en faveur du débat public. L’objectif est clair : offrir aux citoyens une plateforme pour interpeller directement les élus sur des sujets d’intérêt national. Chaque mardi, des questions envoyées par les lecteurs sont sélectionnées par la rédaction et adressées aux responsables concernés. item.description: Dans la commune de Conlie, près du Mans, notre compatriote Mylène Sookahet-Lebegue vient d’être brillamment élue au conseil municipal, dès le premier tour. item.description: Le ton adopté vendredi dernier par plusieurs dirigeants du MMM à la suite de la démission de Paul Bérenger comme *Deputy Prime Minister* (DPM) était une réaction «humaine» et «émotionnelle». C’est ce qu’a soutenu Reza Uteem, président du parti, lors du point de presse tenu hier, à l’issue de la réunion du BP. item.title: Tensions politiques : La crise au sein du MMM et ce qu’elle signifie pour le cœur et l’âme de la démocratie à Maurice
item.description: Le débat public à Maurice ces derniers temps m’a profondément attristé pour mon pays. Il est devenu plus qu’évident pour moi, au cours des derniers jours, que les Mauriciens, de manière générale, ne comprennent pas ce que sont les principes de la démocratie, ce qu’ils signifient réellement et comment ils s’appliquent. Pour moi, cela signifie malheureusement qu’il n’y aura probablement pas de véritable changement à Maurice avant plusieurs générations — du moins t ...

item.title: Au cœur de la crise mauve
item.description: Le MMM traverse aujourd’hui une crise de celles qui ne surgissent pas par accident. Elles s’installent, sédimentent, se taisent… avant de revenir brûler ce qu’elles n’avaient fait qu’entamer. Ce n’est pas une simple querelle d’ego entre 16 élus dressés contre les deux Bérenger. C’est une répétition historique. Une combustion lente dont l’origine remonte, au moins, à 1993. En août 1993, Paul Bérenger est évincé du gouvernement dirigé par Anerood Jugnauth, lui-m ...

item.title: Tensions à la rue Ambrose : «Vander!» hurle la foule hostile au Bureau politique du MMM
item.description: À Rose-Hill, à la rue Ambrose, hier en fin d’aprèsmidi, l’atmosphère était lourde bien avant le début du Bureau politique (BP) prévu à 17 heures. Dès 16h30, ministres, députés et militants étaient déjà sur place. Des petits groupes discutent à voix basse. On observe, on attend. Puis, les premiers éclats de voix se font entendre. item.title: US Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as homeland security chief
item.description:
[BBC News] The US senator will now oversee the embattled department after a series of immigration enforcement blunders.
[France 24] The US Senate confirmed Markwayne Mullin as homeland security secretary late on Monday, approving President Donald Trump's nominee to take over the embattled department after the firing of Kristi Noem in the wake of a public backlash over the administration's immigration enforcement operations.
item.titles: US Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as homeland security chief US Senate confirms Trump ally Markwayne Mullin as homeland security chief

item.title: Des policiers de l’immigration envoyés en renfort dans quatorze aéroports américains
item.description: Face à la paralysie budgétaire partielle, le gouvernement américain a mis sa menace à exécution. Des agents de la police américaine de l’immigration (ICE) ont été déployés, lundi 23 mars, dans 14 aéroports du pays pour répondre à une pénurie de personnel de sécurité, selon Tom Homan, conseiller de Donald Trump sur l’immigration cité par la chaîne CNN. « Et il y en aura plus », a-t-il ajouté. item.titles: Des policiers de l’immigration envoyés en renfort dans quatorze aéroports américains

item.title: La volte-face de Donald Trump qui évoque désormais des négociations avec l’Iran
item.description: Il y a encore 48 heures, Donald Trump ne voulait pas entendre parler d’un cessez-le-feu. Pourtant, à la surprise générale, ce matin, le président américain annonce des négociations avec l’Iran. « Nous avons des discussions solides avec eux et on va voir où ça nous mène. On va leur laisser cinq jours et si ça se passe bien, on scellera un accord. Sinon, on continuera à bombarder à tout va », a déclaré le président américain. Donald Trump repousse donc ses menaces de frappes sur les centrales élec
item.titles: La volte-face de Donald Trump qui évoque désormais des négociations avec l’Iran

item.title: Kenya: Anger Rises Towards Authorities As Torrential Rains Kill Over 80 People in Kenya
item.description:
[AllAfrica] [RFI] Storms that triggered flash floods in Kenya have killed at least 81 people so far this month, authorities said, as rain continued to pound much of the country. In the capital Nairobi, one of the hardest hit areas, the governor has come under fire for bad planning, with calls for him to resign.
[Africanews] In Kenya, entire villages have been submerged by floodwaters in Kisumu County. The region is bearing the brunt of climate-related hazards. The latest torrential rains have killed more than 80 people, according to authorities

item.description: South Africa on Monday reburied the remains of 63 Khoisan people, among southern Africa's oldest indigenous communities, some of whose bodies were shipped to European museums over a century ago.

item.title: Slovenia becomes first EU country to introduce fuel rationing
item.description: Until further notice, motorists in Slovenia will be restricted to a maximum purchase of 50 litres of fuel per day.

item.title: Kenyan ex-foreign minister arrested and accused of staging his disappearance
item.description:
[BBC Africa] The reported disappearance of Raphael Tuju had led to claims he may have been abducted.
[Africanews] Former Kenyan cabinet minister Raphael Tuju has said he went into hiding after suspecting he was being followed, a day after his disappearance prompted fears he may have been abducted.

item.title: ‘We could deploy $80 million a year through angel investing’ — ABAN CEO on fixing early-stage capital
item.description: Even as global VC funding in Africa slows down, ABAN's local networks keep deals moving. At the centre of all this is Fadilah Tchoumba, ABAN's CEO and the fund manager for Catalytic Africa.

item.title: Africa: Afreximbank and Fci to Host Africa Regional Conference On Factoring, Receivables Finance &amp; Credit Insurance in Kampala
item.description: [Afreximbank] Cairo/Amsterdam -- African Export-Import Bank (Afreximbank) and FCI will jointly host the Africa Regional Conference on Factoring, Receivables Finance & Credit Insurance on 15-16 April 2026 in Kampala, Uganda. As Africa moves towards a unified market, these financial tools serve as essential catalysts for bridging the trade finance gap, particularly for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and accelerating intra-African trade volumes.
item.titles: Africa: Afreximbank and Fci to Host Africa Regional Conference On Factoring, Receivables Finance &amp; Credit Insurance in Kampala

item.title: Ruling party candidate Wadagni lays out platform ahead of Benin's presidential campaign
item.description: With one week to go before the official launch of the presidential campaign, Romuald Wadagni, the ruling coaltion's candiate and President Patrice Talon’s designated successor, unveiled his vision for the country on Saturday in Cotonou.
item.titles: Ruling party candidate Wadagni lays out platform ahead of Benin's presidential campaign

item.title: Benin: Benin's Economic Architect Wadagni Launches Visionary Bid to Unleash Youth Potential and Regional Wealth
item.description: [InfoWire] Former Finance Minister Romuald Wadagni, the driving force behind Benin's impressive 8% economic expansion, has officially launched his 2026 presidential campaign with a bold promise: transforming the nation's macroeconomic triumph into everyday prosperity. His "Further, Together" manifesto sets out an optimistic, forward-looking agenda to harness the untapped energy of Benin's youth and decentralize wealth creation across all regions.
item.titles: Benin: Benin's Economic Architect Wadagni Launches Visionary Bid to Unleash Youth Potential and Regional Wealth

item.title: Uganda: Funding Shortfalls Push Uganda's Refugees Deeper Into Hunger
item.description: [WFP] The East African nation has long offered asylum to some of the continent's most vulnerable people. item.titles: Uganda: Funding Shortfalls Push Uganda's Refugees Deeper Into Hunger
